title: monitoring — health probes
description: This module OWNS health checks — the module's own boot-time health probes are a small set of self-consistency checks (schema migrations applied, provider drivers registered, provider schemas loaded, evaluator running, retention pipeline recent). The tenant-facing HealthCheck rows are USER health checks (not module boot probes).

probes.name: monitoring-schema-migrations-applied
probes.description: Confirms every migration this module ships has run: health_checks, health_check_runs, monitoring_alert_policies, monitoring_alerts, monitoring_incidents, monitoring_provider_configs, plus their indexes (composite unique partial indexes on tenant_id + slug/name; retention-sweep indexes on created_at / next_retry_at).

probes.name: monitoring-provider-drivers-registered
probes.description: Confirms every advertised provider (via #[AsMonitoringProvider]) is registered with MonitoringProviderManager + its createXxxDriver factory resolves without errors. Fails hard when a driver class is missing.

probes.name: monitoring-provider-schemas-loaded
probes.description: Confirms every data/providers/<provider>-config.schema.json is present + parseable. Missing schemas would make provider config create silently accept invalid credentials.

probes.name: monitoring-check-type-catalogs-loaded
probes.description: Confirms data/check-type-catalog.json + data/signal-type-catalog.json + data/severity-catalog.json parse + reference every enum value in the HealthCheckType / MonitoringSignalType / MonitoringSeverity enums.

probes.name: monitoring-executor-strategies-registered
probes.description: Confirms every HealthCheckType has a registered HealthCheckExecutor strategy (via #[AsHealthCheckStrategy]). Missing a strategy would make RunHealthCheckJob throw at first invocation.

probes.name: monitoring-evaluator-recent
probes.description: Confirms EvaluateAlertPoliciesJob completed within the last 5 minutes. Stale evaluator indicates the scheduler drifted or the queue backed up. Non-critical because a stale evaluator delays alert firing but doesn't cause data loss.

probes.name: monitoring-firing-alerts-backlog
probes.description: Warns when > 100 alerts are in status='firing' for > 30 minutes across all tenants. Indicates alert overload OR notification-provider outage OR incident-grouper failure.

probes.name: monitoring-open-incidents
probes.description: Counts open incidents by severity. Feeds the platform ops dashboard. Not critical per-se — an open P1 is expected during outages.

probes.name: monitoring-open-circuit-breakers
probes.description: Counts monitoring_provider_configs with circuit_breaker_state='open'. Non-zero means at least one tenant × provider has notification dispatch degraded. Every open breaker over 24h is a P1 signal — the monitoring is telling ops the monitoring is broken.

probes.name: monitoring-notification-failure-rate
probes.description: Trailing 15-minute per-provider notification failure rate. > 10% warns; > 25% alerts.

probes.name: monitoring-retention-recent
probes.description: Confirms PurgeExpiredHealthCheckRunsJob + PurgeResolvedIncidentsJob completed within the last 24h + 7d respectively. Stale retention = disk growth (fixable ops task).

probes.name: monitoring-encryption-key-rotated
probes.description: Warns when the encryption key used for monitoring_provider_configs.config has not been rotated in > 90 days. Delegated to the compliance/encryption service's rotation cadence.
